{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/261977334?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kodareef5/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kodareef5/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kodareef5/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kodareef5/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/kodareef5", "id": 261977334, "login": "kodareef5", "node_id": "U_kgDOD5109g", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kodareef5/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kodareef5/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kodareef5/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kodareef5/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kodareef5/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/kodareef5", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": 4.0, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 4.0, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-178", "name": "Improper Handling of Case Sensitivity"}], "description": "`DelegatedRole._is_target_in_pathpattern` uses `fnmatch.fnmatch` to decide whether a given target path is authorized by a delegation's glob pattern.

Python's `fnmatch.fnmatch` calls `os.path.normcase()` on both arguments before matching. On POSIX hosts `normcase` is the identity function; on Windows hosts `os.path` resolves to `ntpath`, whose `normcase` lowercases its input and replaces `/` with `\`.

As a result, python-tuf's delegation *path pattern* matching is case-sensitive on Linux/macOS but case-INSENSITIVE on Windows. This makes the authorization decision for a target dependent on the host operating system of the client running the updater.

The result on Windows is a TUF specification violation in the python-tuf `ngclient` implementation.

## Vulnerable code

`tuf/api/_payload.py` (HEAD `7ecb67d`):

```python
1183  @staticmethod
1184  def _is_target_in_pathpattern(targetpath: str, pathpattern: str) -> bool:
1185      """Determine whether ``targetpath`` matches the ``pathpattern``."""
1186      # We need to make sure that targetpath and pathpattern are pointing to
1187      # the same directory as fnmatch doesn't threat "/" as a special symbol.
1188      target_parts = targetpath.split("/")
1189      pattern_parts = pathpattern.split("/")
1190      if len(target_parts) != len(pattern_parts):
1191          return False
1192
1193      # Every part in the pathpattern could include a glob pattern, that's why
1194      # each of the target and pathpattern parts should match.
1195      for target, pattern in zip(target_parts, pattern_parts, strict=True):
1196          if not fnmatch.fnmatch(target, pattern):
1197              return False
1198      return True
```

`fnmatch.fnmatch` source (Python 3.12, unchanged in current mainline):

```python
def fnmatch(name, pat):
    ...
    name = os.path.normcase(name)
    pat = os.path.normcase(pat)
    return fnmatchcase(name, pat)
```

## Fix

Replace `fnmatch.fnmatch` with `fnmatch.fnmatchcase`, which is explicitly documented as "not applying case normalization", so it behaves identically across platforms.

## Attack

1. A TUF repository with two path-based delegations whose patterns differ only in case — for example, `Foo/*` and `foo/*`.
2. The "attacker" delegation is listed BEFORE the "legit" delegation in the delegation order.
3. The client searches for `foo/something`: on Windows, it will find the "attacker" provided target "Foo/something".


## Exploitability caveats 

* The attack needs a repository configuration with case-colliding delegation path patterns. The attacker must control one of the delegated roles.
* Delegation ordering matters: the attacker-controlled role must be visited BEFORE the legit role in the pre-order walk.
* The client must run on Windows. No effect on Linux/macOS.

## Credit

Reporter: Koda Reef @kodareef5 
Advisory edits: Jussi Kukkonen @jku", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-qp9x-wp8f-qgjj", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-28T22:46:13Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-qp9x-wp8f-qgjj", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-qp9x-wp8f-qgjj"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-05-28T22:46:13Z", "references": ["https://github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf/security/advisories/GHSA-qp9x-wp8f-qgjj", "https://github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf/releases/tag/v7.0.0", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-qp9x-wp8f-qgjj"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/theupdateframework/python-tuf/security-advisories/GHSA-qp9x-wp8f-qgjj", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/theupdateframework/python-tuf", "summary": "tuf has platform-dependent delegation path matching", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-28T22:46:14Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-qp9x-wp8f-qgjj", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "7.0.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "tuf"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 6.0.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: tuf has platform-dependent delegation path matching
